seoTitle: Rocky Hills vs Rokeby — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Rocky Hills and Rokeby. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Rocky Hills vs Rokeby: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Rocky Hills and Rokeby.
heading: Rocky Hills vs Rokeby — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Rocky Hills, Tasmania and Rokeby, Tasmania. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Rokeby has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Rocky Hills (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Rocky Hills or Rokeby safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Rocky Hills has a flood risk score of 38/100, while Rokeby scores 42/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Rocky Hills scores 47/100 for bushfire risk. Rokeby scores 29/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Rocky Hills or Rokeby?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Rokeby has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.13% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
